summary:
IC Funded scored 79/100 during testing, placing it among the top-rated prop firms. You can choose between a one step or two step challenge, with the funded profit split set at 80% (80/20 in favour of the trader) after the third payout has been processed.

All trading at IC Funded is conducted in a simulated environment. You'll trade on MT5 with IC Markets-style execution. While scaling is capped at $500K and weekend holding is restricted on the 1-Step programme, the structured challenges and broker-backed conditions make it a solid choice. IC Funded does not provide services in the United States, Canada, and other restricted jurisdictions.
bestFor: Traders who prioritise broker-backed execution and structured evaluation challenges
notIdealFor: Traders in the US or Canada, or those who need high profit splits or scaling beyond $500K

categorySections.challenges:
IC Funded offers both **one step** and **two step** challenges. The one step has a single **10% profit target** with **3% daily drawdown**, **6% max drawdown**, **3 minimum trading days**, and a **45% consistency rule**. Weekend holding is not allowed on 1-Step.

The two step splits across **10% and 5% targets**. Step 1 has **4% daily drawdown** and **8% max drawdown**; Step 2 has **5% daily drawdown** and **10% max drawdown** for $5k–$50k accounts, or **8% max drawdown** for $100k–$200k. Each step requires **3 minimum trading days**. Step 1 includes a soft 45% consistency rule. 2-Step traders may hold positions over the weekend.

1-Step account sizes are **$10,000 to $200,000** with fees from **$154**. 2-Step sizes are **$5,000 to $200,000** with fees from **$74**. The funded profit split is **80%** (80/20), applied after the third payout. All trading is conducted in a simulated environment.

Funded stage drawdowns differ by programme: 1-Step has **3% daily** and **7% max drawdown**; 2-Step has **5% daily** and **10% max drawdown**. Scaling is performance-based up to $500K. IC Funded scored 8/10 for challenges.
categorySections.spreads:
IC Funded is connected to **IC Markets**, and traders benefit from IC Markets-style raw pricing on the MT5 platform. Specific spread and commission figures have not been independently verified from official IC Funded documentation.

IC Funded scored 9/10 for spreads based on the expectation of competitive raw-style pricing through the IC Markets connection.
categorySections.markets:
IC Funded provides access to **forex**, **indices**, and **commodities** on the MT5 platform. Leverage for FX majors is confirmed at **1:50 for 2-Step** and **1:30 for 1-Step**. Detailed leverage by asset class has not been independently verified from official IC Funded sources.

IC Funded scored 9/10 for markets — solid access to core instruments with broker-backed execution.
categorySections.platforms:
IC Funded supports **MetaTrader 5 (MT5)** as its trading platform. MT5 offers advanced charting, multiple timeframes, additional order types, an integrated economic calendar, and support for automated trading through Expert Advisors.

EAs are permitted only if they do not copy trades and the trader owns or controls the source logic. Copy trading is not permitted. IC Funded scored 8/10 for platforms.
categorySections.payouts:
The funded profit split is **80%** (80/20 in favour of the trader). The move to 80/20 happens after the **third payout** has been processed. Payouts follow a **bi-weekly schedule** (every 14 calendar days).

First payout eligibility requires a minimum of **14 calendar days**, at least **5 profitable trading days**, and each profitable trade must be at least **0.5%** of the account balance. A profitable day is any trading day that closes with a net positive balance increase of at least 0.5% of the account value. The funded-stage minimum withdrawal is at least **1%** of the account balance.

The challenge fee is refunded with the **third profit share payout**, provided there is a profit share. Withdrawals are processed via bank wire, Skrill, or crypto. KYC is required before progression to the funded stage. IC Funded scored 9/10 for payouts.
categorySections.support:
IC Funded provides **24/7 customer support** through AI-assisted live chat, with email support for more complex matters. No direct contact number is available.

Educational resources include curated videos on market trends and trading strategies accessible through the client dashboard, covering platform navigation, market analysis, and broader financial topics.
categorySections.trust:
IC Funded is connected to **IC Markets**, a well-established broker regulated by **ASIC** in Australia and **CySEC** in Europe. IC Funded Evaluations LTD is incorporated in **Saint Lucia**. While IC Funded itself is not a regulated broker, the IC Markets connection provides a trust foundation.

TrustPilot score is **2.7/5** with mixed reviews — many lower ratings come from users still in evaluation or waiting on first payouts rather than funded traders. IC Funded does not provide services in the United States, Canada, and other restricted jurisdictions.

verdictBlocks.heading: Final Verdict on IC Funded
verdictBlocks.body:
IC Funded is a broker-backed prop firm offering both one step and two step evaluations with fees starting from $74 (2-Step) and $154 (1-Step), with account sizes up to $200,000. All trading is conducted in a simulated environment. You trade on MT5 with IC Markets-style execution and a funded profit split of 80% (applied after the third payout).

The challenge structure differs meaningfully between programmes. The 1-Step has tighter risk limits (3% daily drawdown, 6% max, 45% consistency rule, no weekend holding) while the 2-Step provides more room (up to 5% daily drawdown and 10% max drawdown, weekend holding allowed). Funded-stage drawdowns also vary: 3% daily and 7% max for 1-Step; 5% daily and 10% max for 2-Step.

Scaling is performance-based up to $500K. IC Funded does not provide services in the United States, Canada, and other restricted jurisdictions. Overall, IC Funded is a solid option for traders who want broker-backed conditions and a clear evaluation structure.

faq.q: What is the evaluation process for IC Funded?
faq.a: IC Funded offers a one step or two step challenge. The one step has a 10% profit target with 3% daily drawdown, 6% max drawdown, 3 minimum trading days, and a 45% consistency rule. The two step has 10% and 5% targets across two phases, with 4% daily drawdown and 8% max drawdown in Step 1, and 5% daily drawdown and up to 10% max drawdown in Step 2. Each step requires 3 minimum trading days. All trading is in a simulated environment.

faq.q: What is the profit split structure at IC Funded?
faq.a: The funded profit split is 80% (80/20 in favour of the trader). The move to 80/20 happens after the third payout has been processed. Payouts are bi-weekly, every 14 calendar days.

faq.q: What platforms are available with IC Funded?
faq.a: IC Funded supports MetaTrader 5 (MT5). EAs are permitted only if they do not copy trades and the trader owns or controls the source logic. Copy trading is not permitted.

faq.q: Does IC Funded accept US or Canadian traders?
faq.a: No. IC Funded does not provide services in the United States, Canada, and other restricted jurisdictions.
